chapter_question: How does angular excess become Euler characteristic bookkeeping?
inspection_targets: Face-by-face excess accumulates into 2 pi chi.
V minus E plus F survives across polyhedra.
Cutting a face changes the visible net but not the accounting logic.
All interior edge contributions cancel in the final sum.

source_span.focus: Proves global Gauss-Bonnet through triangulations, Euler characteristic, and angular excess bookkeeping.
source_span.label: Chapter 18
source_span.title: Second (Angular Excess) Proof of the Global Gauss-Bonnet Theorem
topics: Euler characteristic as V - E + F
Polygonal nets and their Euler characteristic
Legendre's proof of the polyhedral formula
